reason: The statement applies because Joshua, initially known as Oshea, was one of the men sent by Moses to spy out the land of Canaan. He, along with Caleb, expressed faith in the Lord's promise and encouraged the Israelites to possess the land, contrasting with the majority who doubted and spoke against the land. Joshua, due to his faith and loyalty to God, was chosen to lead the Israelites into the promised land after the death of Moses.
